steps.title: Understand what margin of error means
steps.content: A margin of error defines the interval around a sample statistic where the true population parameter is expected to fall. We construct the confidence interval by adding and subtracting the margin from the point estimate.

steps.title: Calculate the confidence interval
steps.content:
With point estimate 32% and margin of error 3.41%, the confidence interval is:

Lower bound: $32\% - 3.41\% = 28.59\%$

Upper bound: $32\% + 3.41\% = 35.41\%$

steps.title: Match to the correct choice
steps.content:
Choice A claims more than 35.41%, exceeding our upper bound.

Choices C and D incorrectly reject all conclusions, misunderstanding that a random sample provides valid population estimates.

Choice B states the interval between 28.59% and 35.41%, which matches our confidence interval exactly.

That matches choice B — B is correct.
